title_original: A robot arm with puppy dog eyes is just one of Lenovo’s new desktop AI concepts
description: Alongside a handful of new laptop concepts (and a range of real products too), Lenovo used MWC to announce a pair of AI-based productivity companion concepts. Both are standalone desk devices designed to boost productivity while providing office workers with a bit of artificial dystopic companionship. Lenovo describes its AI Workmate Concept as an "always-on […]
title_he: A robot arm with puppy dog eyes is just one of Lenovo’s new desktop AI concepts
summary_he: Lenovo has unveiled a pair of AI-based productivity companion concepts, including the AI Workmate Concept, a robot arm with a screen displaying puppy dog eyes. This device can scan documents, project presentations, and provide office workers with a bit of artificial companionship. It can be controlled via voice commands and physical gestures.
details_he: The AI Workmate Concept is a standalone desk device designed to boost productivity and provide office workers with a companion. It features a robotic arm that can extend and hover over physical documents to scan them, and a projector to display documents on the desk or nearby wall. The device also includes a camera that can be used to scan notes and documents, generating summaries and organizing ideas. Lenovo's AI Workmate Concept is part of its new desktop AI concepts, which aim to enhance office productivity and provide a bit of artificial companionship.

title_original: Google looks to tackle longstanding RCS spam in India — but not alone
description: Google is integrating carrier-level filtering into RCS in India through a partnership with Airtel to strengthen protections against spam.
title_he: Google looks to tackle longstanding RCS spam in India — but not alone
summary_he: Google is working with Indian telecom operator Bharti Airtel to combat spam on its Rich Communication Services (RCS) platform in India. The partnership aims to integrate Airtel's network-level spam filtering into the RCS ecosystem, enhancing protections against unwanted messages and fraud. This move comes as complaints about spam on RCS have persisted in India.
details_he: India has become a challenging market for spam and fraud due to its vast mobile user base, rapid growth in digital payments, and aggressive marketing practices. Google temporarily paused business promotions on RCS in India in 2022 due to significant complaints about unsolicited ads. Despite this, users continue to report frustration with spam messages on Google Messages, highlighting the ongoing issue. The partnership between Google and Airtel aims to strengthen protections against spam and fraud through real-time checks on business messaging, including sender verification and spam detection.

title_original: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
description: By CEO Sam Altman’s own admission, OpenAI’s deal with the Department of Defense was “definitely rushed,” and “the optics don’t look good.”
title_he: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
summary_he: OpenAI has faced scrutiny over its agreement with the Pentagon after Anthropic's deal fell through, with CEO Sam Altman admitting the process was 'definitely rushed'. OpenAI has since published a blog post outlining its approach to ensuring its models are not used in mass domestic surveillance, autonomous weapons, or high-stakes automated decisions. The company claims its agreement provides stronger safeguards than other AI companies.
details_he: OpenAI's agreement with the Pentagon has been met with skepticism due to the rushed nature of the negotiations. In contrast, Anthropic's deal with the Pentagon fell through after President Donald Trump directed federal agencies to stop using the company's technology. OpenAI's blog post highlights its approach to protecting its red lines, including retaining full discretion over its safety stack, deploying via cloud, and having strong contractual protections. This approach is seen as more expansive and multi-layered compared to other AI companies.

title_original: Anthropic’s Claude rises to No. 1 in the App Store following Pentagon dispute
description: Anthropic’s chatbot Claude seems to have benefited from the attention around the company’s fraught negotiations with the Pentagon.
title_he: Anthropic’s Claude rises to No. 1 in the App Store following Pentagon dispute
summary_he: Anthropic's chatbot Claude has surged to the top of the US App Store following a high-profile dispute with the Pentagon. The company's negotiations with the Department of Defense over safeguards for its AI models appear to have generated significant attention and interest in its product. Claude has broken daily sign-up records and seen a significant increase in users since January.
details_he: Claude's rapid ascent to the number one spot in the App Store is attributed to the controversy surrounding Anthropic's negotiations with the Pentagon. The company's attempts to secure safeguards against mass domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons led to a ban on its products by the Department of Defense. OpenAI, a rival company, has since announced its own agreement with the Pentagon, which includes similar safeguards. Anthropic's spokesperson reported a significant increase in users, with daily sign-ups breaking records and free users rising by over 60% since January.

title_original: SaaS in, SaaS out: Here’s what’s driving the SaaSpocalypse
description: What's behind the SaaSpocalypse? It simply seems a new supreme has risen.
title_he: SaaS in, SaaS out: Here’s what’s driving the SaaSpocalypse
summary_he: The rise of AI-powered tools is disrupting the traditional SaaS business model, making it easier for companies to build their own software and potentially rendering the per-seat pricing model obsolete.
details_he: The increasing use of AI agents, such as Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex, is allowing companies to replicate the core functions of SaaS products and even create their own alternatives, putting downward pressure on SaaS vendors' contracts and pricing.

title_original: Letting Machines Decide What Matters
description: In the time it takes you to read this sentence, theLarge Hadron Collider(LHC) will have smashed billions of particles together. In all likelihood, it will have found exactly what it found yesterday: more evidence to support theStandard Modelof particle physics.For the engineers who built this 27-kilometer-long ring, this consistency is a triumph. But for theoretical physicists, it has been rather frustrating. AsMatthew Hutsonreports in “AI Hunts for the Next Big Thing in Physics,” the field is currently gripped by a quiet crisis. In an email discussing his reporting, Hutson explains that the Standard Model, which describes the known elementary particles and forces, is not a complete picture. “So theorists have proposed new ideas, and experimentalists have built giant facilities to test them, but despite the gobs of data, there have been no big breakthroughs,” Hutson says. “There are key components of reality we’re completely missing.”That’s why researchers are turning artificial intell
title_he: Letting Machines Decide What Matters
summary_he: Researchers in particle physics are struggling to find new evidence beyond the Standard Model, leading to a quiet crisis in the field. Theorists have proposed new ideas, but experimental results have been inconsistent. To address this, scientists are exploring the use of artificial intelligence to analyze data and identify potential breakthroughs.
details_he: The Standard Model, which describes known elementary particles and forces, is incomplete and has not led to significant breakthroughs despite large amounts of data and experimental efforts. Researchers are turning to artificial intelligence to analyze data and identify potential new physics beyond the Standard Model. This approach may help address the quiet crisis in the field and lead to a deeper understanding of reality.

title_original: HumanMCP: A Human-Like Query Dataset for Evaluating MCP Tool Retrieval Performance
description:
arXiv:2602.23367v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers contain a collection of thousands of open-source standardized tools, linking LLMs to external systems; however, existing datasets and benchmarks lack realistic, human-like user queries, remaining a critical gap in evaluating the tool usage and ecosystems of MCP servers. Existing datasets often do contain tool descriptions but fail to represent how different users portray their requests, leading to poor generalization and inflated reliability of certain benchmarks. This paper introduces the first large-scale MCP dataset featuring diverse, high-quality diverse user queries generated specifically to match 2800 tools across 308 MCP servers, developing on the MCP Zero dataset. Each tool is paired with multiple unique user personas that we have generated, to capture varying levels of user intent ranging from precise task requests, and ambiguous, exploratory commands, reflecting the complexity of real-world
title_he: HumanMCP: A Human-Like Query Dataset for Evaluating MCP Tool Retrieval Performance
summary_he: Researchers have created a new dataset called HumanMCP to evaluate the performance of Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool retrieval systems. This dataset features diverse, high-quality user queries that mimic real-world interactions with MCP servers. The queries are designed to capture varying levels of user intent, from precise task requests to ambiguous commands.
details_he: The HumanMCP dataset addresses a critical gap in evaluating MCP tool usage and ecosystems by providing realistic, human-like user queries. Existing datasets often contain tool descriptions but fail to represent how different users portray their requests, leading to poor generalization and inflated reliability of certain benchmarks. The new dataset includes 2800 tools across 308 MCP servers, paired with multiple unique user personas to capture the complexity of real-world interaction patterns.

title_original: Toward General Semantic Chunking: A Discriminative Framework for Ultra-Long Documents
description:
arXiv:2602.23370v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Long-document topic segmentation plays an important role in information retrieval and document understanding, yet existing methods still show clear shortcomings in ultra-long text settings. Traditional discriminative models are constrained by fixed windows and cannot model document-level semantics; generative large language models can output paragraph boundaries, but inference is expensive and long inputs are difficult to support. To address these issues, we propose a discriminative segmentation model based on Qwen3-0.6B. On top of the backbone network, we add a cross-window context fusion layer and a boundary classification head, and combine them with an overlapping sliding-window strategy. Our model supports single-pass inputs of up to 13k tokens and can be extended to ultra-long documents for paragraph boundary detection. To further enhance downstream retrieval efficiency, we derive a vector fusion method with scalar correction, which
title_he: Toward General Semantic Chunking: A Discriminative Framework for Ultra-Long Documents
summary_he: Researchers have developed a new framework for segmenting ultra-long documents into meaningful chunks, addressing limitations of existing methods in information retrieval and document understanding.
details_he: The proposed framework, called Toward General Semantic Chunking, uses a discriminative model based on Qwen3-0.6B and incorporates a cross-window context fusion layer and a boundary classification head. This allows for single-pass inputs of up to 13k tokens and can be extended to ultra-long documents for paragraph boundary detection. Experiments on the Wikipedia long-document topic segmentation dataset WIKI-727K showed that the method achieved better macro-averaged F1 and delivered two orders of magnitude faster inference compared to generative models.

title_original: An Agentic LLM Framework for Adverse Media Screening in AML Compliance
description:
arXiv:2602.23373v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Adverse media screening is a critical component of anti-money laundering (AML) and know-your-customer (KYC) compliance processes in financial institutions. Traditional approaches rely on keyword-based searches that generate high false-positive rates or require extensive manual review. We present an agentic system that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to automate adverse media screening. Our system implements a multi-step approach where an LLM agent searches the web, retrieves and processes relevant documents, and computes an Adverse Media Index (AMI) score for each subject. We evaluate our approach using multiple LLM backends on a dataset comprising Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs), persons from regulatory watchlists, and sanctioned persons from OpenSanctions and clean names from academic sources, demonstrating the system's ability to distinguish between high-risk and low-risk individuals
title_he: An Agentic LLM Framework for Adverse Media Screening in AML Compliance
summary_he: Researchers have developed an agentic system using Large Language Models to automate adverse media screening in anti-money laundering compliance. This system can distinguish between high-risk and low-risk individuals by computing an Adverse Media Index score. The approach has been evaluated using multiple LLM backends on a dataset of Politically Exposed Persons and others.
details_he: The traditional keyword-based searches used in adverse media screening often result in high false-positive rates or require extensive manual review. The new agentic system leverages Retrieval-Augmented Generation to automate the process. It searches the web, retrieves and processes relevant documents, and computes an Adverse Media Index score for each subject. This approach has the potential to improve the efficiency and accuracy of adverse media screening in financial institutions.

title_original: Task-Lens: Cross-Task Utility Based Speech Dataset Profiling for Low-Resource Indian Languages
description:
arXiv:2602.23388v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rising demand for inclusive speech technologies amplifies the need for multilingual datasets for Natural Language Processing (NLP) research. However, limited awareness of existing task-specific resources in low-resource languages hinders research. This challenge is especially acute in linguistically diverse countries, such as India. Cross-task profiling of existing Indian speech datasets can alleviate the data scarcity challenge. This involves investigating the utility of datasets across multiple downstream tasks rather than focusing on a single task. Prior surveys typically catalogue datasets for a single task, leaving comprehensive cross-task profiling as an open opportunity. Therefore, we propose Task-Lens, a cross-task survey that assesses the readiness of 50 Indian speech datasets spanning 26 languages for nine downstream speech tasks. First, we analyze which datasets contain metadata and properties suitable for specific tasks.
title_he: Task-Lens: Cross-Task Utility Based Speech Dataset Profiling for Low-Resource Indian Languages
summary_he: Researchers have developed a tool called Task-Lens to help address the challenge of limited speech datasets in low-resource Indian languages. This tool assesses the readiness of existing datasets for various downstream speech tasks and identifies areas where more resources are needed. By unlocking the potential of existing datasets, Task-Lens aims to support more inclusive speech technologies.
details_he: The tool, Task-Lens, profiles 50 Indian speech datasets spanning 26 languages for nine downstream speech tasks. It analyzes the datasets' metadata and properties to determine their suitability for specific tasks and proposes enhancements to unlock their full potential. The researchers' findings reveal that many existing datasets contain untapped metadata that can support multiple tasks, highlighting the need for more resources in certain areas.

title_original: Detoxifying LLMs via Representation Erasure-Based Preference Optimization
description:
arXiv:2602.23391v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) trained on webscale data can produce toxic outputs, raising concerns for safe deployment. Prior defenses, based on applications of DPO, NPO, and similar algorithms, reduce the likelihood of harmful continuations, but not robustly so: they are vulnerable to adversarial prompting and easily undone by fine-tuning-based relearning attacks. Indeed, research has shown that these edits to the model are superficial: linear probing reveals that harmful "directions" remain present in representations. To address this, we propose Representation Erasure-based Preference Optimization (REPO), reformulating detoxification as a token-level preference problem. Using a novel objective with preference data, we force the representations of toxic continuations to converge toward their benign counterparts. Our mechanistic analysis reveals that this granular approach is critical: unlike baselines, REPO induces deep, localized edits
title_he: Detoxifying LLMs via Representation Erasure-Based Preference Optimization
summary_he: Researchers have proposed a new method to detoxify large language models (LLMs) by reformulating detoxification as a token-level preference problem. This approach, called Representation Erasure-based Preference Optimization (REPO), forces the representations of toxic continuations to converge toward their benign counterparts. REPO achieves state-of-the-art robustness against sophisticated threats, including relearning attacks and enhanced GCG jailbreaks.
details_he: The proposed method, REPO, uses a novel objective with preference data to induce deep, localized edits to toxicity-encoding neurons while preserving general model utility. Unlike existing representation- and output-based methods, REPO is robust against adversarial prompting and fine-tuning-based relearning attacks. The method has been shown to be effective in stopping sophisticated threats, including relearning attacks and enhanced GCG jailbreaks. This research has significant implications for the safe deployment of LLMs.

title_original: U-CAN: Utility-Aware Contrastive Attenuation for Efficient Unlearning in Generative Recommendation
description:
arXiv:2602.23400v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Generative Recommendation (GenRec) typically leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to redefine personalization as an instruction-driven sequence generation task. However, fine-tuning on user logs inadvertently encodes sensitive attributes into model parameters, raising critical privacy concerns. Existing Machine Unlearning (MU) techniques struggle to navigate this tension due to the Polysemy Dilemma, where neurons superimpose sensitive data with general reasoning patterns, leading to catastrophic utility loss under traditional gradient or pruning methods. To address this, we propose Utility-aware Contrastive AttenuatioN (U-CAN), a precision unlearning framework that operates on low-rank adapters. U-CAN quantifies risk by contrasting activations and focuses on neurons with asymmetric responses that are highly sensitive to the forgetting set but suppressed on the retention set. To safeguard performance, we introduce a utility-aware calibr
title_he: U-CAN: Utility-Aware Contrastive Attenuation for Efficient Unlearning in Generative Recommendation
summary_he: Researchers have developed a new framework called U-CAN to address the issue of sensitive data being encoded into model parameters in generative recommendation systems. This framework, which operates on low-rank adapters, aims to balance the need for unlearning sensitive data with the need to preserve model performance. U-CAN uses a utility-aware calibration mechanism to selectively down-scale high-risk parameters.
details_he: The proposed framework, U-CAN, aims to mitigate the Polysemy Dilemma, where neurons superimpose sensitive data with general reasoning patterns, leading to catastrophic utility loss. It does this by contrasting activations and focusing on neurons with asymmetric responses that are highly sensitive to the forgetting set but suppressed on the retention set. U-CAN also introduces a utility-aware calibration mechanism that combines weight magnitudes with retention-set activation norms to assign higher utility scores to dimensions that contribute strongly to retention performance.

title_original: Long Range Frequency Tuning for QML
description:
arXiv:2602.23409v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Quantum machine learning models using angle encoding naturally represent truncated Fourier series, providing universal function approximation capabilities with sufficient circuit depth. For unary fixed-frequency encodings, circuit depth scales as O(omega_max * (omega_max + epsilon^{-2})) with target frequency magnitude omega_max and precision epsilon. Trainable-frequency approaches theoretically reduce this to match the target spectrum size, requiring only as many encoding gates as frequencies in the target spectrum. Despite this compelling efficiency, their practical effectiveness hinges on a key assumption: that gradient-based optimization can drive prefactors to arbitrary target values. We demonstrate through systematic experiments that frequency prefactors exhibit limited trainability: movement is constrained to approximately +/-1 units with typical learning rates. When target frequencies lie outside this reachable range, optimizatio
title_he: Long Range Frequency Tuning for QML
summary_he: Researchers have developed a new approach to improve the performance of quantum machine learning models by introducing a grid-based initialization method, which helps to overcome a limitation in the trainability of frequency prefactors.
details_he: The limitation in trainability of frequency prefactors in quantum machine learning models was demonstrated through systematic experiments, which showed that frequency prefactors can only be moved by approximately +/-1 units with typical learning rates. This limitation can lead to optimization failure when target frequencies lie outside the reachable range. The proposed grid-based initialization method using ternary encodings generates dense integer frequency spectra, ensuring that target frequencies lie within the locally reachable range and achieving better performance compared to the trainable-frequency baseline.

title_original: Brain-OF: An Omnifunctional Foundation Model for fMRI, EEG and MEG
description:
arXiv:2602.23410v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Brain foundation models have achieved remarkable advances across a wide range of neuroscience tasks. However, most existing models are limited to a single functional modality, restricting their ability to exploit complementary spatiotemporal dynamics and the collective data scale across imaging techniques. To address this limitation, we propose Brain-OF, the first omnifunctional brain foundation model jointly pretrained on fMRI, EEG and MEG, capable of handling both unimodal and multimodal inputs within a unified framework. To reconcile heterogeneous spatiotemporal resolutions, we introduce the Any-Resolution Neural Signal Sampler, which projects diverse brain signals into a shared semantic space.To further manage semantic shifts, the Brain-OF backbone integrates DINT attention with a Sparse Mixture of Experts, where shared experts capture modality-invariant representations and routed experts specialize in modality-specific semantics. Fu
title_he: Brain-OF: An Omnifunctional Foundation Model for fMRI, EEG and MEG
summary_he: Researchers have developed Brain-OF, an omnifunctional foundation model that can process multiple types of brain signals, including fMRI, EEG, and MEG, to improve performance on various neuroscience tasks.
details_he: Brain-OF is the first model to be jointly pretrained on these three imaging techniques, allowing it to handle both single and multiple input signals within a unified framework. To address the differences in signal resolution, the model uses a neural signal sampler and integrates DINT attention with a sparse mixture of experts. The model also employs a dual-domain pretraining objective to reconstruct brain signals in both the time and frequency domains.

title_original: EvoX: Meta-Evolution for Automated Discovery
description:
arXiv:2602.23413v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent work such as AlphaEvolve has shown that combining LLM-driven optimization with evolutionary search can effectively improve programs, prompts, and algorithms across domains. In this paradigm, previously evaluated solutions are reused to guide the model toward new candidate solutions. Crucially, the effectiveness of this evolution process depends on the search strategy: how prior solutions are selected and varied to generate new candidates. However, most existing methods rely on fixed search strategies with predefined knobs (e.g., explore-exploit ratios) that remain static throughout execution. While effective in some settings, these approaches often fail to adapt across tasks, or even within the same task as the search space changes over time. We introduce EvoX, an adaptive evolution method that optimizes its own evolution process. EvoX jointly evolves candidate solutions and the search strategies used to generate them, continuousl
title_he: EvoX: Meta-Evolution for Automated Discovery
summary_he: Researchers have developed EvoX, a meta-evolution method that adapts its search strategy to optimize the discovery of new solutions. This approach outperforms existing AI-driven evolutionary methods in nearly 200 real-world optimization tasks.
details_he: EvoX continuously updates its search strategy based on progress, allowing it to dynamically shift between different strategies during the optimization process. This adaptability enables EvoX to improve upon existing methods, including AlphaEvolve, OpenEvolve, GEPA, and ShinkaEvolve, in a wide range of tasks.

title_original: Truncated Step-Level Sampling with Process Rewards for Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning
description:
arXiv:2602.23440v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Training large language models to reason with search engines via reinforcement learning is hindered by a fundamental credit assignment problem: existing methods such as Search-R1 provide only a sparse outcome reward after an entire multi-step trajectory, making it infeasible to attribute success or failure to individual reasoning and retrieval decisions. Process-reward methods like StepSearch alleviate this by introducing step-level supervision, but rely on heuristic rewards such as TF-IDF overlap with gold documents, and still sample k complete trajectories per example, retaining high gradient variance. We propose SLATE, a framework built on two complementary ideas: (1) truncated step-level sampling, which generates k trajectories that share a common prefix and differ only at the next step, and (2) dense LLM-as-judge rewards, which replace heuristic scoring with a capable LLM evaluator that assesses the quality of each reasoning step, s
title_he: Truncated Step-Level Sampling with Process Rewards for Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning
summary_he: Researchers have developed a new framework called SLATE to improve training of large language models for reasoning with search engines. This framework uses truncated step-level sampling and dense rewards to reduce the variance of policy gradients and improve performance. Experiments on seven question-answering benchmarks show that SLATE outperforms existing methods, particularly on harder tasks and smaller models.
details_he: The new framework, SLATE, addresses the credit assignment problem in training large language models by introducing truncated step-level sampling and dense rewards. This approach reduces the variance of advantage estimates and improves the targetedness of policy gradients. The experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of SLATE in improving performance on question-answering tasks, especially on harder tasks and smaller models.

title_original: ClawJacked attack let malicious websites hijack OpenClaw to steal data
description: Security researchers have disclosed a high-severity vulnerability dubbed "ClawJacked" in the popular AI agent OpenClaw that allowed a malicious website to silently bruteforce access to a locally running instance and take control over it. [...]
title_he: ClawJacked attack let malicious websites hijack OpenClaw to steal data
summary_he: A high-severity vulnerability in OpenClaw, a self-hosted AI platform, allowed malicious websites to silently brute-force access to locally running instances and take control over them. This was possible due to the OpenClaw gateway service binding to localhost by default and exposing a WebSocket interface. A fix was released in version 2026.2.26 on February 26.
details_he: The vulnerability, dubbed 'ClawJacked', was discovered by Oasis Security and affects OpenClaw users who visit malicious websites. The issue is caused by browser cross-origin policies not blocking WebSocket connections to localhost, allowing attackers to silently open a connection to the local gateway and attempt authentication. The researchers found that they could brute-force the OpenClaw management password at hundreds of attempts per second without failed attempts being throttled or logged, making it possible to gain control over the instance in a short amount of time.

title_original: Samsung TVs to stop collecting Texans’ data without express consent
description: Samsung and the State of Texas have reached a settlement agreement over the alleged unlawful collection of content-viewing information through its smart TVs [...]
title_he: Samsung TVs to stop collecting Texans’ data without express consent
summary_he: Samsung has agreed to revise its privacy disclosures after a Texas lawsuit alleged the company collected viewing data without consent. The lawsuit claimed Samsung used Automated Content Recognition (ACR) technology to capture screenshots of consumers' TVs for targeted advertising. The company will make changes to strengthen its privacy disclosures.
details_he: The lawsuit, filed by Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, claimed Samsung used 'dark patterns' to automatically enroll customers in its system, requiring over 200 clicks to read privacy statements. A temporary restraining order was issued, but later vacated. Samsung maintains its original policy followed existing Texas state regulations, but has agreed to make enhancements to its privacy disclosures.

title_original: Hackers Weaponize Claude Code in Mexican Government Cyberattack
description: The AI was abused to write exploits, create tools, and automatically exfiltrate over 150GB of data.The postHackers Weaponize Claude Code in Mexican Government Cyberattackappeared first onSecurityWeek.
title_he: Hackers Weaponize Claude Code in Mexican Government Cyberattack
summary_he: A recent cyberattack on the Mexican government leveraged the AI model Claude to automate malicious activities, including data exfiltration.
details_he: Hackers exploited Claude's capabilities to write exploits, create tools, and automatically transfer over 150GB of sensitive data, highlighting the potential risks of AI-powered cyber threats.

title_original: ISC Stormcast For Monday, March 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9830, (Mon, Mar 2nd)
description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.
title_he: ISC Stormcast For Monday, March 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9830, (Mon, Mar 2nd)
summary_he: The SANS Internet Storm Center has released its weekly podcast for March 2nd, 2026, providing insights into the latest cybersecurity threats and trends.
details_he: The ISC Stormcast offers a comprehensive analysis of current cybersecurity issues, offering valuable information for IT professionals and security enthusiasts to stay informed and protect their networks.

title_original: The Post-Quantum Journey Begins: Enforce, See, and Evolve with Quantum-Safe SASE
description: Encrypted data has a shelf life, and for many organizations it must remain secret for years. The post-quantum risk is not a dramatic collapse of encryption, but a quieter threat: attackers harvesting encrypted traffic today so they can decrypt it in the future. That is why post-quantum readiness is increasingly a board and CEO-level responsibility,...The postThe Post-Quantum Journey Begins: Enforce, See, and Evolve with Quantum-Safe SASEappeared first onCato Networks.
title_he: The Post-Quantum Journey Begins: Enforce, See, and Evolve with Quantum-Safe SASE
summary_he: As quantum computing advances, organizations must prepare for the potential threat of post-quantum attacks, which involve attackers harvesting encrypted data today to decrypt it in the future, posing a risk to long-term business stability and trust.
details_he: The post-quantum risk is a growing concern for CISOs, who must lead the effort to ensure their organizations' long-term security. One practical step is to implement TLS 1.3, which provides post-quantum-cryptography-ready key establishment. However, rolling out this protection across a complex enterprise requires centralized visibility and enforcement, which is where Quantum-Safe SASE comes in, providing a foundation to coordinate cryptographic upgrades and apply them gradually.

title_original: What Defenders Need to Know about Iran’s Cyber Capabilities
description: With the current Iran crisis at its peak, cyber activity is a relevant part of the threat picture alongside kinetic and political pressure. Iran’s ecosystem includes multiple clusters aligned with state entities, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS), as well as deniable operators and “hacktivist” groups. This ecosystem supports a broad set of objectives: espionage to gain intelligence and footholds; disruption and destructive activity, including DDoS attacks, pseudo-ransomware, and data wipers to impose costs; and information operations that pair destructive activity or data leaks with coordinated online amplification. This activity is expected to intensify and broaden across the Middle East, the United States, and […]The postWhat Defenders Need to Know about Iran’s Cyber Capabilitiesappeared first onCheck Point Blog.
title_he: What Defenders Need to Know about Iran’s Cyber Capabilities
summary_he: Iran's cyber capabilities pose a significant threat to multiple countries, including the US, as part of the ongoing crisis. The country's cyber ecosystem includes state entities, deniable operators, and hacktivist groups. These actors use various tactics to achieve espionage, disruption, and information operations goals.
details_he: Iran's cyber activity is expected to intensify and broaden across the Middle East, the US, and other countries viewed as opponents. Key Iranian-linked threat actor clusters, such as Cotton Sandstorm, have been identified and their tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) analyzed. Defenders should watch for early warning signals and implement mitigations to counter these threats.

title_original: טילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץ
description: כשהדאשבורד של AWS נצבע באדום וערוצי ה-Slack בהיסטריה, הראשון שאנחנו מאשימים (לפחות בראש) זה כנראה ג'וניור תורן שדחף קוד לא בדוק לפרודקשן או איש DevOps שעשה טעות קונפיגורציה קריטית ב-DNS, אבל לפחות הפעם, זו לא אשמת הג'וניור. שירותי הענן של אמזון (AWS) קרסו במספר איזורים, לאחר שאחד מהדאטה-סנטרים של החברה באיחוד האמירויות נפגע כתוצאה מהלחימה […]The postטילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: טילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץ
summary_he: כשהדאשבורד של AWS נצבע באדום וערוצי ה-Slack בהיסטריה, הראשון שאנחנו מאשימים (לפחות בראש) זה כנראה ג'וניור תורן שדחף קוד לא בדוק לפרודקשן או איש DevOps שעשה טעות קונפיגורציה קריטית ב-DNS, אבל לפחות הפעם, זו לא אשמת הג'וניור. שירותי הענן של אמזון (AWS) קרסו במספר איזורים, לאחר שאחד מהדאטה-סנטרים של החברה באיחוד האמירויות נפגע כתוצאה מהלחימה […]The postטילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץappeared first onגיקטיים.

title_original: הפנטגון השתמש ב-Claude לצורך התקיפה באיראן
description: ביום שישי האחרון היינו עדים לשתי דרמות שמתחוללות במקביל. הראשונה כללה הכרזת מלחמה של הממשל האמריקאי על חברת ה-AI הפופולרית Anthropic; והשנייה, כללה מלחמה של ממש, כאשר ארה"ב וישראל פתחו במבצע צבאי נרחב באיראן. עכשיו, חושף דיווח של ה-WSJ את האירוניה מאחורי הקלעים. כש-AI ה"Woke" מגיע לשדה הקרב הבלאגן החל ביום שישי האחרון, כשטראמפ הורה […]The postהפנטגון השתמש ב-Claude לצורך התקיפה באיראןappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: הפנטגון השתמש ב-Claude לצורך התקיפה באיראן
summary_he: הפנטגון השתמש ב-Claude, מודל AI של Anthropic, לצורך התקיפה באיראן, על אף שהממשל האמריקאי הכריז על Anthropic כסיכון ביטחוני.
details_he: הפנטגון השתמש ב-Claude לניהול המבצע באיראן, כולל ערכות מודיעיניות, זיהוי מטרות וסימולציה של תרחישי לחימה. הדבר נעשה על אף שהממשל האמריקאי הכריז על Anthropic כסיכון ביטחוני, וטען שהחברה סירבה לתת לפנטגון 'צ'ק פתוח' לשימוש ב-LLM's שלה. הפנטגון נאלץ להקצות שישה חודשים לתהליך של ההוצאה ההדרגתית משימוש של Claude.

title_original: אז מה עשו הסטארטאפים הישראלים רגע לפני המערכה מול איראן
description: בצל המתיחות הביטחונית ורגע לפני סבב הלחימה הנוכחי מול איראן, סיכמו הסטארטאפים הישראלים את חודש פברואר עם נתונים מעודדים. למרות שסכום הגיוסים הכולל מהווה כמחצית מהסכום שגויס בינואר האחרון, מדובר בנתון חיובי מאוד בהשוואה לחודשים המקבילים בשנים עברו. במהלך החודש דיווחו 22 סטארטאפים על גיוסי הון בסך מצרפי של 776 מיליון דולר, כאשר רוב הצ'קים […]The postאז מה עשו הסטארטאפים הישראלים רגע לפני המערכה מול איראןappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: אז מה עשו הסטארטאפים הישראלים רגע לפני המערכה מול איראן
summary_he: בפברואר 2026, סטארטאפים ישראלים גייסו 776 מיליון דולר, כאשר רוב הגיוסים היו לחברות בשלבים מוקדמים. הגיוסים היו חיוביים בהשוואה לחודשים המקבילים בשנים עברו.
details_he: 22 סטארטאפים דיווחו על גיוסי הון בפברואר, כאשר Vega ו-Tomorrow.io היו אחראיות על כ-300 מיליון דולר מהסכום החודשי. Vega גייסה 120 מיליון דולר, ו-Tomorrow.io גייסה 300 מיליון דולר. חמש עסקאות מרכזיות התרחשו בחודש, במסגרתן נרכשו שבעה סטארטאפים ישראליים.

title_original: התקיפה באיראן: מי הרוויח חצי מיליון דולר בפולימרקט?
description: פלטפורמת Polymarket (פולימרקט) אוהבת להגדיר את עצמה כ"שוק חיזויים", מה שכמובן נשמע הרבה יותר אלגנטי מאתר הימורים. רק שבניגוד לקזינו המסורתי, כאן לא מהמרים על רולטה, אלא על אירועים בעולם האמיתי – מפוליטיקה ועד מבצעים צבאיים. בחודשים האחרונים הפלטפורמה עלתה לכותרות בישראל בהקשרים פחות מחמיאים, בעקבות כתב האישום נגד גורמים במערכת הביטחון שהימרו על אירועים […]The postהתקיפה באיראן: מי הרוויח חצי מיליון דולר בפולימרקט?appeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: התקיפה באיראן: מי הרוויח חצי מיליון דולר בפולימרקט?
summary_he: בפלטפורמת Polymarket, שמכונה 'שוק חיזויים', המהמרים ניסו לנחש את תאריך התקיפה האמריקאית באיראן. היקף ההימורים הכולל חצה את רף חצי מיליארד הדולר, וכ-90 מיליון דולר הונחו על תאריך ספציפי אחד: ה-28 בפברואר.
details_he: חברת המחקר Bubblemaps גילתה ששישה חשבונות 'ברי מזל' זיהו נכון את מועד התקיפה וגרפו יחד רווח נקי של כ-1.2 מיליון דולר. החשבונות הללו נפתחו רק בחודש שעבר, והם עשו הימורים קטנים ולא מוצלחים על תאריכים שונים, עד שהחליטו 'להתאבד' דווקא על ה-28 בפברואר.

title_original: "כולם יעשו את זה": מייסד טוויטר מפטר 4,000 עובדים בגלל ה-AI
description: בעוד חברות מנצלות את ה-AI כתירוץ כדי להשלים סבבי פיטורים בשלבים ובהדרגה, מגיעה חברה אחת שמעדיפה לשלוח הביתה 4,000 עובדים במכה אחת. האם זוהי תחילתה של מגמה? בינתיים, המשקיעים מאוד מרוצים מההחלטה. כמעט חצי מהחברה ביום אחד מנכ"ל ומייסד חברת Block, ג'ק דורסי (ממייסדי Twitter), הודיע בסוף השבוע האחרון כי החברה עומדת לפטר יותר מ-4,000 […]The post"כולם יעשו את זה": מייסד טוויטר מפטר 4,000 עובדים בגלל ה-AIappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: "כולם יעשו את זה": מייסד טוויטר מפטר 4,000 עובדים בגלל ה-AI
summary_he: Jack Dorsey, the co-founder of Twitter and CEO of Block, has announced that the company will lay off nearly half of its workforce, around 4,000 employees, in an effort to automate more work using AI.
details_he: The move is seen as a bold step in the tech industry, where companies often use AI as a reason to lay off employees in phases. Block, formerly known as Square, is a fintech company that provides payment solutions for small businesses and is also the developer of Cash App, a popular mobile payment service. The company's CEO, Jack Dorsey, stated that the decision was made to 'be true to where we are today' and to take advantage of the opportunity to work with smaller, more skilled teams that use AI to automate more work.

title_original: "העזרה הגיעה": ישראל פרצה לאפליקציה האיראנית הפופולרית
description: תחילת מבצע "שאגת הארי" אתמול (ש') כללה התראות רבות בישראל, לצד תקיפות ברחבי איראן של הכוחות הישראליים והאמריקאיים. דיווח חדש מגלה כי לצד התקיפות הללו, פעלה ישראל מאחורי הקלעים כדי לפנות לעם האיראני. "משמרות המהפיכה והבסיג' ספגו מכה ניצחת" הוול סטריט ג'ורנל טוען כי בזמן מכת הפתיחה, שכללה את חיסולו של המנהיג העליון של איראן […]The post"העזרה הגיעה": ישראל פרצה לאפליקציה האיראנית הפופולריתappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: "העזרה הגיעה": ישראל פרצה לאפליקציה האיראנית הפופולרית
summary_he: ישראל פרצה לאפליקציה האיראנית BadeSaba Calendar, שהורדה מיליוני פעמים, ושלחה התראות לאזרחים האיראנים. ההתראות כללו הודעות לצבא האיראני להתפרק מנשקו. הפריצה הייתה חלק ממבצע 'שאגת הארי' של ישראל.
details_he: הפריצה לאפליקציה BadeSaba Calendar התרחשה בזמן מבצע 'שאגת הארי', שכלל תקיפות ברחבי איראן. האפליקציה, שהורדה מיליוני פעמים, שימשה כדרך לשלוח התראות לאזרחים האיראנים. ההתראות כללו הודעות לצבא האיראני להתפרק מנשקו. ישראל לא אישרה את הפריצה, אך היא נחשבת לחלק מהסטרטגיה שלה לייצר הרתעה בכמה חזיתות.

title_original: רם מכנס מונה למנכ"ל הבא של ארבה
description: ייכנס לתפקיד ב-1 באפריל. המנכ"ל הנוכחי, קובי מרנקו, ישמש כנשיא החברה. הגדירה אסטרטגיה חדשה המרחיבה את שוקי היעד וכוללת כניסה לשוק הביטחוני. נסחרת בנסד"ק לפי שווי שוק של כ-100 מיליון דולר
title_he: רם מכנס מונה למנכ"ל הבא של ארבה
summary_he: ייכנס לתפקיד ב-1 באפריל. המנכ"ל הנוכחי, קובי מרנקו, ישמש כנשיא החברה. הגדירה אסטרטגיה חדשה המרחיבה את שוקי היעד וכוללת כניסה לשוק הביטחוני. נסחרת בנסד"ק לפי שווי שוק של כ-100 מיליון דולר

title_original: הפנטגון מאיים על עתיד כוכבת ה-AI הזוהרת ביותר
description: משרד ההגנה הכריז שאנתרופיק מהווה “סיכון לשרשרת האספקה”, יום לפני שצבא ארה"ב השתמש בטכנולוגיה שלה כדי לתקוף את איראן. התיוג החריג הזה מאיים על עתיד חברת ה-AI המבטיחה ביותר בארה"ב
title_he: הפנטגון מאיים על עתיד כוכבת ה-AI הזוהרת ביותר
summary_he: The Pentagon has issued a warning to Anthropic, a leading AI company, signaling its potential threat to the US national security supply chain. This unprecedented move targets a US-based company, a departure from previous actions taken against foreign entities like Huawei and ZTE. The warning casts a shadow on Anthropic's ability to expand its services to government agencies and the private sector.
details_he: The Pentagon's decision is a significant development, marking a shift from a routine contract cancellation to a formal designation of Anthropic as a potential security risk. This move is unprecedented in its scope, as it targets a US-based company, rather than foreign entities like Huawei and ZTE. The warning may damage Anthropic's reputation and hinder its ability to expand its services to government agencies and the private sector, which could have significant implications for its future growth.

title_original: טראמפ אסר שימוש בקלוד, אך הוא כיכב במתקפה על איראן
description: שעות לאחר שהנשיא הורה להפסיק את ההתקשרויות עם החברה ולהגדירה כסיכון בשרשרת האספקה, נעשה שימוש במודל קלוד בתקיפה באיראן • במקביל חתמה OpenAI על הסכם חדש עם הפנטגון, בצל ביטול החוזים עם אנתרופיק
title_he: טראמפ אסר שימוש בקלוד, אך הוא כיכב במתקפה על איראן
summary_he: Despite ordering a halt in interactions with the company, a model developed by Meta's subsidiary, Meta AI, was used in a military operation against Iran. This move comes as OpenAI has signed a new agreement with the Pentagon, following the cancellation of contracts with Anthropic.
details_he: The use of the model, known as CLU, or CLoud, in the military operation raises questions about the potential consequences of using AI in such contexts. The cancellation of contracts with Anthropic and the signing of a new agreement with the Pentagon suggest a shift in the way the US government approaches AI development and deployment. The use of AI in military operations also highlights the need for clear guidelines and regulations surrounding the use of such technology.

title_original: קיבלתם סמס מפיקוד העורף? תיזהרו - מדובר בקישור זדוני
description: מערך הסייבר הלאומי מזהיר מהודעות SMS המתחזות לפיקוד העורף ומכילות קישורים זדוניים. הקישורים מפנים להורדת אפליקציות מזיקות שעלולות לאפשר גישה למידע אישי • המערך ממליץ: לא ללחוץ על קישורים, לעדכן אפליקציות רק דרך Google Play ו-App Store, ולבדוק עדכונים רק בערוצים הרשמיים של פיקוד העורף • במקרה של פעילות חשודה ניתן לפנות למוקד 119
title_he: קיבלתם סמס מפיקוד העורף? תיזהרו - מדובר בקישור זדוני
summary_he: Israel's National Cyber Directorate has issued a warning about fake SMS messages claiming to be from the Home Front Command, which contain malicious links. These links lead to the download of harmful apps that may allow access to personal information. The directorate advises against clicking on links and to only update apps through official channels.
details_he: The National Cyber Directorate is cautioning the public to be aware of these fake messages, which are designed to trick recipients into downloading malicious apps. These apps can potentially compromise personal data and put users at risk. The directorate recommends updating apps only through the Google Play Store or App Store, and to check for updates only through the official channels of the Home Front Command. If suspicious activity is detected, users can contact the 119 hotline.

title_original: אין לכם קליטה בממ"ד? זה מה שאפשר לעשות
description: כשנמצאים זמן רב בממ"ד, רבים עשויים להתקשות עם הקליטה הסלולרית • מנקודת גישה בתוך הממ"ד, דרך מתאם רשת חשמל ועד טכנולוגיית ה-Mesh - אלו הפתרונות שקיימים בשוק וכדאי להכיר
title_he: אין לכם קליטה בממ"ד? זה מה שאפשר לעשות
summary_he: Many individuals struggle with cellular reception in crowded areas, such as universities, where they spend extended periods of time. To address this issue, various solutions have been developed, including in-building wireless networks and mesh technology. These solutions aim to improve cellular coverage and connectivity.
details_he: The challenges of cellular reception in crowded areas, such as universities, can be significant. In-building wireless networks and mesh technology are two solutions that have been developed to address this issue. These solutions can improve cellular coverage and connectivity, making it easier for individuals to stay connected. The development of these solutions highlights the ongoing efforts to improve cellular infrastructure and connectivity.

title_original: בזמן ההפצצות: מתקפת הסייבר באפליקציית התפילות הפופולרית באיראן
description: במקביל לגל התקיפות, מיליוני איראנים קיבלו דרך אפליקציית תפילות מסרים המבטיחים חנינה לחיילים שיניחו את נשקם • בישראל שומרים על עמימות, אך ההערכה היא כי מדובר במבצע מתוכנן היטב
title_he: בזמן ההפצצות: מתקפת הסייבר באפליקציית התפילות הפופולרית באיראן
summary_he: While Iran is under attack, a popular prayer app has been sending millions of Iranians messages promising forgiveness to soldiers who abandon their weapons. The Israeli government is maintaining a neutral stance, but experts believe this is a well-planned operation.
details_he: The prayer app, which is extremely popular in Iran, has been sending out messages to millions of users, promising forgiveness to soldiers who abandon their weapons during the current attacks. This has raised concerns about the potential impact on the conflict. The Israeli government has not commented on the matter, but experts believe that the operation is well-planned and coordinated.
